title: Goods at Standard Rate (Registered Buyer)
description: This applies to the sale of goods subject to the standard 18% sales tax rate made to sales tax registered buyers. These buyers can claim input tax credits.

title: Goods at Standard Rate (Unregistered Buyer)
description: When goods are sold to buyers not registered for sales tax (B2C sales), the seller charges full sales tax. The buyer cannot claim input tax credits.

title: Steel Melting and Re-rolling
description: The steel sector is governed by strict traceability and sector-specific rules. Tax authorities regulate their tax treatment distinctly, sometimes applying specific tax rates.

title: Ship Breaking
description: Ship breakers dismantle old ships and recover scrap steel. This scrap is treated separately for tax purposes with special rates or exemptions.

title: Goods at Reduced Rate (Eighth Schedule)
description: Certain goods are taxed at a reduced rate (lower than standard) to encourage affordability. The Eighth Schedule lists these goods – commonly basic food items, medicines, or essential commodities.

title: Exempt Goods (Sixth Schedule)
description: Goods listed in the Sixth Schedule are exempt from sales tax. This exemption reduces tax burden on essential or socially important items.

title: Zero-Rated Goods
description: Zero-rated goods have sales tax charged at 0%. The seller can claim input tax credits on purchases. This is often applied to exported goods.

title: Third Schedule Goods (Retail Price Based)
description: Items under the Third Schedule are subject to sales tax based on printed retail price rather than transaction value.

title: Cotton Ginners
description: Purchases from registered cotton ginners are subject to specific rules under cotton trade taxation, possibly involving reverse charge mechanisms.

title: Telecommunication Services
description: Mobile operators providing telecom services are taxed under specific rules, including additional regulatory fees with both FED and GST.

title: Toll Manufacturing
description: Toll manufacturing involves third-party processing of raw materials into finished products. The processor issues an invoice for conversion charges.

title: Petroleum Products
description: Petroleum products like petrol, diesel, or lubricants have distinct sales tax rates or federal excise duties due to their economic importance.

title: Electricity Supply to Retailers
description: Selling electricity to retailers has unique tax implications with different rates for wholesale vs retail.

title: Gas to CNG Stations
description: Natural gas sold to CNG filling stations may have special tax treatment to promote cleaner fuels.

title: Mobile Phones
description: Mobile handsets attract standard sales tax but may be subject to additional duties or regulatory charges.

title: Processing/Conversion of Goods
description: Services where raw materials are converted into finished products through manufacturing or processing.

title: Goods (FED in ST Mode)
description: Federal Excise Duty collected through the sales tax system, streamlining collection.

title: Services (FED in ST Mode)
description: Certain services like advertisement, franchise, insurance are liable to FED but invoiced under sales tax framework.

title: ICT Services
description: Businesses providing services such as consultancy, software development, and IT solutions are taxed under distinct ordinance.

title: Electric Vehicles
description: Electric vehicles may be incentivized through reduced sales tax rates to encourage environmentally friendly transportation.

title: Cement/Concrete Block
description: Cement and concrete blocks are taxed at standard rate and subject to strict regulation due to environmental impact.

title: Potassium Chlorate
description: A sensitive chemical used in matchstick manufacturing, subject to fixed tax per kilogram.

title: CNG Sales
description: Sales of Compressed Natural Gas involve regulated pricing and specific tax treatments including both FED and sales tax.

title: Goods as per SRO.297(I)/2023
description: Specific goods subject to reduced, conditional, or fixed-rate taxation under this SRO.

title: Non-Adjustable Supplies (Pharmaceuticals)
description: Certain pharmaceutical products taxed at fixed rate under serial 81 of Eighth Schedule to make medicines affordable.

title: Retailer - Standard Rate Goods
description: Retailers selling taxable goods directly to end consumers through POS systems integrated with FBR's IRIS platform.

title: Retailer - Third Schedule Goods
description: Retailers selling goods under 3rd Schedule must charge tax based on maximum retail price (MRP).

title: Retailer - Reduced Rate Goods
description: Essential goods like baby milk and books subject to reduced tax rate to keep vital products affordable.
